subject: Fix certain default values breaking VFC
commit_message:
Fix certain default values breaking VFC

For string default values in VFC, only alphanumeric characters should be allowed

Issue-ID: SDC-4475
Signed-off-by: eschcam <cameron.scholes@est.tech>
Change-Id: Id4ca2943f1423b8cc7d3182254298ba19c8a7ee3

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-2

I believe the issue here is the solitary single quote which is causing the value to be non-parsable as either json or yaml. A similar problem would occur for a solitary ", {, [
The current solution is too restrictive (e.g. cant create a regex in a regex constraint).
If we check that these don't occur singularity then I think it should be ok

Patch Set 5:

This is still causing valid patterns in pattern constraint to be rejected.
Looking into it closer I think we can solve it in backend (which has added benefit of protecting against same error when user is using APIs rather then UI)
In PropertyConvertor::convertToToscaObject we try to parse the value as json, if that fails we try to parse it as yaml (in the catch block). 
We could expand this that if the type is string and cannot be parsed as yaml, just return the string value as is

Patch Set 7:

Latest patch does not seem to follow the suggestion in comment on patch set 5. If there is a reason for the different approach please add reply to the comment to detail

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> This is still causing valid patterns in pattern constraint to be rejected.
> Looking into it closer I think we can solve it in backend (which has added benefit of protecting against same error when user is using APIs rather then UI)
> In PropertyConvertor::convertToToscaObject we try to parse the value as json, if that fails we try to parse it as yaml (in the catch block). 
> We could expand this that if the type is string and cannot be parsed as yaml, just return the string value as is

I have a partial implementation of this.

convertToToscaObject fails to generate a JSON object when using a non-terminated string.
My assumption is that if the JSON object cannot be created then the string value that has been used is an invalid string that should be accepted and instead an error should be returned

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> > Patch Set 5:
> > 
> > This is still causing valid patterns in pattern constraint to be rejected.
> > Looking into it closer I think we can solve it in backend (which has added benefit of protecting against same error when user is using APIs rather then UI)
> > In PropertyConvertor::convertToToscaObject we try to parse the value as json, if that fails we try to parse it as yaml (in the catch block). 
> > We could expand this that if the type is string and cannot be parsed as yaml, just return the string value as is
> 
> I have a partial implementation of this.
> 
> convertToToscaObject fails to generate a JSON object when using a non-terminated string.
> My assumption is that if the JSON object cannot be created then the string value that has been used is an invalid string that should be accepted and instead an error should be returned

No, that is not the wanted behaviour. Want to accept values unless there is a reason not to. In this case it can be accepted by using the string value as is for a string property, so no need to reject (unless I am missing something). We need to be as permissive as possible in order to support e.g. patterns, custom tosca functions.
